title: ptilotus nobilis subsp. semilanatus
intro: Ptilotus nobilis subsp. semilanatus, commonly known as woolly heads, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ptilotus nobilis subsp. semilanatus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
